channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Strategy STRC Offering Hits Record High in Single Day

item.title: Crypto holders in France are being violently targeted again — and it’s no longer just insiders
item.description: A French couple held at knifepoint in their home near Versailles and forced to transfer roughly €900,000 in Bitcoin would normally read like a rare, tragic story. But in France, it now fits a pattern ...
item.dc:creator: CryptoSlate

item.description: XRP futures traders appear to be pulling back as open interest dropped, funding rates weakened, and exchange transaction activity fell significantly....
item.dc:creator: CryptoPotato

item.title: MediaTek patches bug enabling crypto seed theft in just 45 seconds
item.description: Ledger’s white-hat security team said it found a flaw in MediaTek's secure boot chain that can be used to steal sensitive information from certain Android devices....
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph - Security

item.title: INC Ransomware Group Holds Healthcare Hostage in Oceania
item.description: Government agencies, emergency clinics, and others in Australia, New Zealand, and Tonga have had serious run-ins with the prolific ransomware outfit....

item.title: Xygeni GitHub Action Compromised Via Tag Poison
item.description: Attackers operated an active C2 implant for up to a week and compromised AppSec vendor Xygeni's xygeni/xygeni-action in that time....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Researchers Trick Perplexity's Comet AI Browser Into Phishing Scam in Under Four Minutes
item.description: Agentic web browsers that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities to autonomously execute actions across multiple websites on behalf of a user could be trained and tricked into falling prey...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports o...
item.dc:creator: Krebs On Security

item.title: Critical n8n Flaws Allow Remote Code Execution and Exposure of Stored Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two now-patched security flaws in the n8n workflow automation platform, including two critical bugs that could result in arbitrary command execution...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Chinese Nexus Actors Shift Focus to Qatar Amid Iranian Conflict
item.description: Two attacks on Qatari entities signal a shift in focus for China-backed actors and demonstrate how quickly they can pivot in response to geopolitical events....

item.title: Meta Disables 150K Accounts Linked to Southeast Asia Scam Centers in Global Crackdown
item.description: Meta on Wednesday said it disabled over 150,000 accounts associated with scam centers in Southeast Asia as part of a coordinated effort in partnership with authorities from Thailand, the U.S., the U.K...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Dozens of Vendors Patch Security Flaws Across Enterprise Software and Network Devices
item.description: SAP has released security updates to address two critical security flaws that could be exploited to achieve arbitrary code execution on affected systems. The vulnerabilities in question listed below -...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: What Boards Must Demand in the Age of AI-Automated Exploitation
item.description: “You knew, and you could have acted. Why didn’t you?” This is the question you do not want to be asked. And increasingly, it’s the question leaders are forced to answer after an incident. For years, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Patches 84 Flaws in March Patch Tuesday, Including Two Public Zero-Days
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for a set of 84 new security vulnerabilities affecting various software components, including two that have been listed as publicly known. Of these, eight are rat...
